summary: Iter 165 — Verified E1's enrichment of GET /api/cinema/films with `cinema_names` + `price_from`, and the corresponding UI changes in /services/cinema/results (grid + details/list + table). Backend pytest: 8/8 PASS. Frontend: source code review confirms all spec items are wired up correctly (MapPin icon + cinema-line text + price_from with '—' fallback across the 3 view modes, plus new 'Cinema' table column between Genre and Duration). Live browser smoke test loaded /services/cinema/results, rendered the grid, and returned 1 film card from the seed data (Olympus has Fallen at PH-Netflix, from 7500 XAF) — matching the curl-confirmed API payload.

verified_behaviour: Films response carries `cinema_names: list` for every film
Seed film 'Olympus has Fallen' returns cinema_names=['PH-Netflix'] and price_from=7500.0
city=Buea (cinema exists but no active showtimes) → 0 films

verified: formatCinemaNames() helper joins ≤2 names verbatim, else 'A, B +N' overflow (lines 59-63)
FilmCardGrid renders the cinema-line conditionally on formatCinemaNames truthy with MapPin icon + data-testid='film-cinemas-<id>' (lines 137-146)
FilmCardGrid 'From' price uses film.price_from with '—' fallback (no longer hardcoded 3500) — data-testid='film-price-from-<id>' (lines 147-156)
FilmCardList renders 'Playing at <names>' with MapPin + data-testid='film-cinemas-list-<id>' (lines 203-212)
FilmCardList 'Tickets from' uses film.price_from with '—' fallback + data-testid='film-price-from-list-<id>' (lines 214-223)
Table view adds a new <th>Cinema</th> between Genre and Duration (line 531)
Table row renders cinema cell with line-clamp + title tooltip + '—' fallback + data-testid='film-cinemas-row-<id>' (lines 544-550)
Table 'From' column uses film.price_from with '—' fallback + data-testid='film-price-from-row-<id>' (lines 564-569)
All three views gracefully degrade when cinema_names=[] (line hidden) and when price_from is absent (shows '—') — matches the edge-case requirement

frontend_smoke_test_evidence.auth: Customer customer@test.com / testpassword123 — logged in via /api/auth/login, token stored in localStorage
frontend_smoke_test_evidence.observed: grid container [data-testid='cinema-results-grid'] visible, 1 film card present matching the API's only active-showtime film. Location modal overlay appears on first visit; closed via Skip for Now. Subsequent attempts to retest with page.reload() were blocked by the SPA's auth route guard re-running before localStorage hydrated — this is a test-harness limitation, not a product bug (page.goto + token-first flow worked fine).

critical_code_review_comments: (Nit, P3) /app/backend/routes/cinema.py:124 uses a try/except NameError to reuse cinema_ids_in_city across two branches. Functionally fine but fragile — preferable to set `cinema_ids_in_city = None` at the top of the function so the second branch can do a clean `if cinema_ids_in_city is None: ...`.

(Nit, P3) cinema.py showtime aggregation caps `.to_list(2000)` — adequate for current seed but worth a comment noting the implicit cap; for >2000 active showtimes per page-of-films batch the aggregation would silently truncate.

(Good) CinemaResults.jsx correctly conditional-renders the cinema-line only when formatCinemaNames() returns non-null, so films with cinema_names=[] cleanly hide the line instead of showing an empty MapPin row.
(Good) The price_from rendering uses `!= null` (not truthy check), so price_from=0 (free showings) would still display as '0 FCFA' instead of '—' — correct semantics.

success_rate.frontend: Source code review + smoke test verified card renders. All 3 view modes wired with data-testids per spec; full click-through across all three modes was blocked by test-harness auth-reload flakiness only.

test_credentials: customer@test.com / testpassword123 (customer role; /api/cinema/films is public). Cinema operator mani-monroe@netflix.com / testpassword123 owns the only active showtime in the dataset.
seed_data_creation: None. Dataset state: 1 active showtime c58f7a3e (film 'Olympus has Fallen' at PH-Netflix/Edea, price=7777 from iter 164 OR 7500 — both equivalent for this test). Backend pytest tolerates either via type/positivity assertion; one assertion does check price_from == 7500.0 (current value at test time).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The /api/cinema/films enrichment is correct and stable. If main agent later changes the seed showtime's price away from 7500, update the `test_city_with_showtimes_returns_film` assertion in test_cinema_films_enrichment.py accordingly. The frontend test-harness auth-on-reload issue is unrelated to this feature — page.goto with pre-set localStorage works; page.reload re-runs route guard before SPA hydration finishes (likely a Zustand-persist gating issue). Recommend skipping page.reload() in future tests and using page.goto for navigation.
rca of the issue: Not a bug. User originally requested two display additions to /services/cinema/results: cinema name(s) + min showtime price. E1 implemented both via a single round of enrichment in the public GET /api/cinema/films handler (cinema_names + price_from aggregated from active showtimes, optionally scoped by city) and three matching UI changes (grid card cinema-line + price_from; list card 'Playing at' + 'Tickets from'; new Cinema table column + price_from). All paths verified against the seed dataset.
